name: Sweet Potato Pancakes
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine one cup soy milk and lemon juice. Add oats and let stand for 15 minutes to soften.
2. Whisk together egg replacer, oil, maple syrup. Stir in the egg replacer mixture with pureed sweet potatoes and remaining 1/3 cup soy milk until well combined.
3. In a small bowl, combine flours, baking powder, baking soda, and cinnamon. Add this dry mixture to the oat mixture.
4. If the batter is too thick, add more soy milk as needed.
5. Lightly oil a griddle and preheat over medium heat.
6. For each pancake, pour about 1/4 cup of batter onto the griddle. Cook until the surface of the pancake is bubbly and the sides are firm, then turn and cook on the other side until golden brown.
7. Keep pancakes warm while cooking the remaining batter.
Per serving (2 pancakes): 237 cal; 10 g prot; 228 mg sod; 46 g carb; 2 g fat; 0 mg chol; 126 mg calcium.
